briefs.title: Proposed: Port Moody Council to Read Territorial Land Acknowledgement at April 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody City Council is set to open its April 21, 2026 meeting with a Territorial Land Acknowledgement — a formal statement recognizing that the city operates on the traditional and unceded (never formally surrendered) homelands of eight Indigenous Peoples, including the Kwikwetlem, Tsleil-Waututh, Musqueam, and Squamish Nations. This is a ceremonial agenda item and does not propose any new rules or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Port Moody agenda for April 21, 2026 Standing Committee meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Council Standing Committee is set to meet on April 21, 2026, to consider several items that could affect local businesses. Topics up for discussion include a civic complex exterior upgrade, a five-year financial plan update, transportation improvements on St. Johns Street and Barnet Highway, contract awards over $200,000, and confirming resolutions related to family-friendly housing units.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 21, 2026 meeting live — decisions on finances and development could affect your business.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council Standing Committee meeting — April 21, 2026

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Finance & Insurance
